subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Each letter in the word DIMPLE is changed to the letter immediately following it in the English alphabetical order and then all the letters thus formed are arranged in alphabetical order. Which of the following letters will be fifth from the right in the new group of letters thus formed?
q: Each letter in the word DIMPLE is changed to the letter immediately following it in the English alphabetical order and then all the letters thus formed are arranged in alphabetical order. Which of the following letters will be fifth from the right in the new group of letters thus formed?
explanation: DIMPLE + 1 -> E, J, N, Q, M, F. Arranging in alphabetical order gives E, F, J, M, N, Q. 5th letter from the right (1st: Q, 2nd: N, 3rd: M, 4th: J, 5th: F) is F.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Six friends, A, B, F, G, H and I are sitting in a straight line facing north. No one sits to the left of G. Only four people sit between G and B. Only three people sit to the left of H. I sits to the immediate left of A. How many people sit between F and G?
q: Six friends, A, B, F, G, H and I are sitting in a straight line facing north. No one sits to the left of G. Only four people sit between G and B. Only three people sit to the left of H. I sits to the immediate left of A. How many people sit between F and G?
explanation: Arrangement from left to right: G - I - A - H - F - B. The number of people sitting between F and G is 3 (I, A, H).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: A person was born on 3 October 2019. What day of the week will be on his 6th birthday?
q: A person was born on 3 October 2019. What day of the week will be on his 6th birthday?
explanation: $3 Oct 2019 was Thursday. Years from 2019 to 2025: 6 years + 2 leap years (2020, 2024) = 8 odd days = 1 odd day mod 7. Thursday + 1 day = Friday$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Which of the following pairs of years have identical calendars?
q: Which of the following pairs of years have identical calendars?
explanation: $Both 1981 and 1987 are ordinary years. Number \text{ of } odd days between 1981 and 1987 is 5 normal + 1 leap (1984) = 7 odd days = 0. Hence they have identical calendars$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: CULE is related to ARHZ in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, YODU is related to WLZP. To which of the given options is UIVK related, following the same logic?
q: CULE is related to ARHZ in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, YODU is related to WLZP. To which of the given options is UIVK related, following the same logic?
explanation: Pattern: 1st letter 2, 2nd letter 3, 3rd letter 4, 4th letter 5. U(2)S, I(3)F, V(4)R, K(5)F ⇒ SFRF.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Find the number of triangles in the given star figure.
q: Find the number of triangles in the given star figure.
explanation: $A standard 6pointed star (hexagram) consists \text{ of } 6 small outer triangles + 2 large overlapping triangles = 8 triangles$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the given equation, if '+' and '-' are interchanged and '×' and '÷' are interchanged?
$15 ÷ 21-22 + 36 × 4 = ?$
q:

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four lettercluster pairs are alike in a certain way. Which is the lettercluster pair that DOES NOT belong to that group?
q: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four lettercluster pairs are alike in a certain way. Which is the lettercluster pair that DOES NOT belong to that group?
explanation: Pattern: 1st letter 1, 2nd letter 1. Q(1)P, N(1)M. K(1)J, H(1)G. M(1)L, J(1)I. For WTUU, W(1)U and T(1)U does not hold.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: Seven boxes J, K, L, M, T, U and V are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only J is kept above L. Only T is kept between L and M. Only V is kept below K. How many boxes are kept between U and V?
q: Seven boxes J, K, L, M, T, U and V are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only J is kept above L. Only T is kept between L and M. Only V is kept below K. How many boxes are kept between U and V?
explanation: Stack order from top (7) to bottom (1): 7. J, 6. L, 5. T, 4. M, 3. U, 2. K, 1. V. Exactly one box (K) is between U and V.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: In a certain code language, 'A + B' means 'A is the daughter of B', 'A # B' means 'A is the mother of B', 'A × B' means 'A is the wife of B', and 'A & B' means 'A is the father of B'. How is S related to T if 'S & G + J # K × T'?
q: In a certain code language, 'A + B' means 'A is the daughter of B', 'A # B' means 'A is the mother of B', 'A × B' means 'A is the wife of B', and 'A & B' means 'A is the father of B'. How is S related to T if 'S & G + J # K × T'?
explanation: S is father of G. G is daughter of J (so S is husband of J). J is mother of K. K is wife of T. S is the father of T's wife K (Wife's father).

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: A, B, C, D, L, M and N are sitting in a straight line facing north. Only two people sit to the left of C. Only three people sit between C and L. Only one person sits between L and M. D sits to the immediate left of A. N is not an immediate neighbour of L. How many people sit to the right of B?
q: A, B, C, D, L, M and N are sitting in a straight line facing north. Only two people sit to the left of C. Only three people sit between C and L. Only one person sits between L and M. D sits to the immediate left of A. N is not an immediate neighbour of L. How many people sit to the right of B?
explanation: Seating order: D - A - C - N - M - B - L. Only one person (L) sits to the right of B.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: I, J, K, L, M, N and O are sitting around a circular table facing the centre of the table. Only two people sit between I and O when counted from the left of O. J sits third to the left of N. L sits to the immediate right of N. L sits second to the left of I. K is not an immediate neighbour of J. Who sits third to the right of M?
q: I, J, K, L, M, N and O are sitting around a circular table facing the centre of the table. Only two people sit between I and O when counted from the left of O. J sits third to the left of N. L sits to the immediate right of N. L sits second to the left of I. K is not an immediate neighbour of J. Who sits third to the right of M?
explanation: Circular arrangement clockwise: N - L - K - I - J - M - O. Counting 3 steps to the right of M gives L.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?
BBU, ECQ, HDM, ?, NFE
q:
What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order?
BBU, ECQ, HDM, ?, NFE
explanation: 1st letter: +3 (B, E, H, K, N). 2nd letter: +1 (B, C, D, E, F). 3rd letter: 4 (U, Q, M, I, E). Result is KEI.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
If 'S' stands for '-', 'T' stands for '×', 'U' stands for '÷' and 'V' stands for '+', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
$18 U 3 V 1 S 7 T 2 = ?$
q:
If 'S' stands for '-', 'T' stands for '×', 'U' stands for '÷' and 'V' stands for '+', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation?
$18 U 3 V 1 S 7 T 2 = ?$
explanation: $Replacing letters with operations: 18 ÷ 3 + 1-7 × 2 = 6 + 1-14 = 7-14 = 7$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: E is the father of G. G is the brother of H. N is the father of L. L is the son of H. How is E related to L?
q: E is the father of G. G is the brother of H. N is the father of L. L is the son of H. How is E related to L?
explanation: E is father of H (since G & H are siblings). H is mother of L (since N is father and L is son). E is L's Mother's father.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
A statement is given followed by two conclusions. Identify which of the given conclusions is/are true based on the given statement.
$Statement: N = K < L ≤ G = B > T$
Conclusions:
I. B > N
II. N < G
q:
A statement is given followed by two conclusions. Identify which of the given conclusions is/are true based on the given statement.
$Statement: N = K < L ≤ G = B > T$
Conclusions:
I. B > N
II. N < G

options.text: Both conclusions I and II are true
Neither conclusion I nor II is true

explanation: $From chain N < L ≤ G = B: N < B ⇒ B > N (Conclusion I holds). N < G (Conclusion II holds). Both conclusions are true$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: S is the mother of R. R is the sister of K. P is the father of B. B is the husband of K. How is S related to P?
q: S is the mother of R. R is the sister of K. P is the father of B. B is the husband of K. How is S related to P?
explanation: P's son is B. B's wife is K. S is mother of K. Thus S is Son's wife's mother of P.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question: If 1 is subtracted from each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 765874925143, what will be the sum of the digits that are second from the left and second from the right in the new number thus formed?
q: If 1 is subtracted from each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 765874925143, what will be the sum of the digits that are second from the left and second from the right in the new number thus formed?
explanation: $New number: 644662804022. 2nd digit from left = 4. 2nd digit from right = 2. Sum = 4 + 2 = 6$.

subject: Reasoning Ability
question:
In this question, a statement is followed by two conclusions, numbered I and II. Find which conclusion is true based on the given statement.
$Statement: A > B = C ≤ D < E > F$
Conclusions:
I. A > F
$II. D = B$
q:
In this question, a statement is followed by two conclusions, numbered I and II. Find which conclusion is true based on the given statement.
$Statement: A > B = C ≤ D < E > F$
Conclusions:
I. A > F
$II. D = B$

options.text: Neither conclusion I nor II is true
Both conclusions I and II are true

explanation: I. No definite relation between A and F. II. B ≤ D means D can be equal to or greater than B. Neither conclusion is definite.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A can do a work in 12 days, and B can do it in 25 days. If they work on it together for 2 days, then what fraction of the work is left?
q: A can do a work in 12 days, and B can do it in 25 days. If they work on it together for 2 days, then what fraction of the work is left?
explanation: $Combined 1 day work = 1/12 + 1/25 = 37/300. Work in 2 days = 37/150. Fraction left = 1-37/150 = 113/150$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A grocer has 55 kg rice, a part of which he sells at 20% profit and the remaining at 75% profit. He gains 26% on the whole. What is the quantity of rice sold at 75% profit?
q: A grocer has 55 kg rice, a part of which he sells at 20% profit and the remaining at 75% profit. He gains 26% on the whole. What is the quantity of rice sold at 75% profit?
explanation: $By allegation ratio \text{ of } parts = (75-26) : (26-20) = 49 : 6. Quantity at 75\% profit = (6 / 55) × 55 = 6 kg$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: How many nonsquare natural numbers lie strictly between 50^2 and 51^2?
q: How many nonsquare natural numbers lie strictly between 50^2 and 51^2?
explanation: $Number \text{ of } nonsquare natural numbers between n^2 and (n+1)^2 is 2n = 2 × 50 = 100$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A container holds 36 litres of a milkwater mixture in the ratio $7:2$. How many litres of water must be added to make the ratio $5:3$?
q: A container holds 36 litres of a milkwater mixture in the ratio 7 : 2. How many litres of water must be added to make the ratio 5 : 3?
explanation: $Initial Milk = 28L, Water = 8L. New ratio 28 / (8 + x) = 5/3 ⇒ 84 = 40 + 5x ⇒ 5x = 44 ⇒ x = 8.8 litres$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: The sum of the present ages of Ram and Krishna is 50 years. Four years ago, their ages were in the ratio of $3:4$. Find the age of Ram after 4 years from now.
q: The sum of the present ages of Ram and Krishna is 50 years. Four years ago, their ages were in the ratio of 3 : 4. Find the age of Ram after 4 years from now.
explanation: $4 years ago sum = 50-8 = 42. Ratio 3:4 ⇒ Ram's past age = 18. Ram's present age = 22. Ram's age in 4 years = 26 years$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: A car travels for 2 hours at 50 km/h and then for 3 hours at 60 km/h. Find the average speed for the total journey.
q: A car travels for 2 hours at 50 km/h and then for 3 hours at 60 km/h. Find the average speed for the total journey.
explanation: $Total distance = 100 + 180 = 280 km. Total time = 5 hours. Average speed = 280 / 5 = 56 km/h$.

q: The product of two prime numbers is 8051. One of them is 83. Find the difference between the two numbers.
explanation: $Second prime = 8051 / 83 = 97. Difference = 97-83 = 14$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Two taps can fill a cistern in 2 hours and 16 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 16 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill half of the empty cistern, if all of them are opened together?
q: Two taps can fill a cistern in 2 hours and 16 hours, respectively. A third tap can empty it in 16 hours. How long (in hours) will it take to fill half of the empty cistern, if all of them are opened together?
explanation: $Net rate per hour = 1/2 + 1/16-1/16 = 1/2. Time for full cistern = 2 hours. Half cistern = 1 hour$.

q:
Improve the highlighted part of the sentence. Choose 'No improvement' if the sentence is grammatically correct.
The sun was beating down mercilessly and there was an urge in the air and an overpowering sense of growth.

options.text: The sun was merciless beating upon
The sun was mercilessly beating through

explanation: 'The sun was beating down mercilessly' is idiomatic and grammatically correct.

subject: English Language
question:
Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence:
The administration must prevent them to access the restricted database.
q:
Choose the most suitable option to replace the highlighted part of the sentence:
The administration must prevent them to access the restricted database.
explanation: Fixed preposition pattern: 'prevent + someone + from + V_ing'.

subject: English Language
question: Choose the correct oneword substitute for: 'A person who hates marriage'.
q: Choose the correct oneword substitute for: 'A person who hates marriage'.
explanation: A 'Misogamist' is a person who dislikes or hates marriage.

subject: English Language
question: Choose the correct oneword substitute for: 'Something mysterious or difficult to understand'.
q: Choose the correct oneword substitute for: 'Something mysterious or difficult to understand'.
explanation: An 'Enigma' is something or someone that is mysterious and difficult to interpret.

subject: English Language
question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: SENTIENT
q: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word: SENTIENT
explanation: 'Sentient' means able to perceive or feel things, synonymous with 'Conscious'.

subject: English Language
question: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: The old woman watered the plants every morning.
q: Choose the correct passive voice transformation of the sentence: The old woman watered the plants every morning.

options.text: The plants was watered by the old woman every morning.
The plants were watered by the old woman every morning.
The plants are watered by the old woman every morning.
The plants had been watered by the old woman every morning.

explanation: Plural object 'The plants' + 'were' + V3 'watered' + by + subject.

subject: English Language
question: What is the correct meaning of the idiom 'A slap on the wrist'?
q: What is the correct meaning of the idiom 'A slap on the wrist'?

options.text: A token reprimand with little consequence

explanation: 'A slap on the wrist' refers to a very mild or light warning/punishment.

subject: English Language
question: Find the correct spelling for a word that means calm under pressure.
q: Find the correct spelling for a word that means calm under pressure.
explanation: 'Equanimity' is the correct spelling for composure under pressure.

subject: English Language
question:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
Rahul has been working in the defence services ________ the last 28 years.
q:
Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.
Rahul has been working in the defence services ________ the last 28 years.
explanation: 'for' is used to denote a duration of time (for the last 28 years).

subject: English Language
question:
Select the correct option:
She tackled every obstacle __ unflinching resolve and composure.
q:
Select the correct option:
She tackled every obstacle __ unflinching resolve and composure.
explanation: Preposition 'with' indicates manner or instrument ('with unflinching resolve').

subject: English Language
question:
Find the part of the sentence that contains an error:
She entered inside (1) / the examination hall (2) / and quietly (3) / took her seat (4).
q:
Find the part of the sentence that contains an error:
She entered inside (1) / the examination hall (2) / and quietly (3) / took her seat (4).
explanation: 'inside' after 'entered' is redundant/superfluous ('entered the examination hall').

subject: English Language
question: What is the official report submitted to the court by the police at the conclusion of an investigation when sufficient evidence is NOT found to prove the crime?
q: What is the official report submitted to the court by the police at the conclusion of an investigation when sufficient evidence is NOT found to prove the crime?
explanation: If police investigation finds insufficient evidence, a Final Report (FR) recommending case closure is submitted.

subject: English Language
question: What is the official document listing suspicious items or evidence seized by police during a raid, prepared at the spot in the presence of witnesses, called?
q: What is the official document listing suspicious items or evidence seized by police during a raid, prepared at the spot in the presence of witnesses, called?
explanation: A 'Seizure Memo' (Fard Baramadgi) is an official inventory prepared on the spot in front of independent witnesses.

subject: English Language
question: What is the legal process called when a court orders government control over the movable or immovable property of an absconder to compel their appearance?
q: What is the legal process called when a court orders government control over the movable or immovable property of an absconder to compel their appearance?
explanation: Under Section 83 CrPC, attachment of property is ordered to compel an absconding offender to surrender.

subject: English Language
question: What is the legal custody granted to police for the transit duration while transferring an accused from one jurisdiction/district to another court called?
q: What is the legal custody granted to police for the transit duration while transferring an accused from one jurisdiction/district to another court called?
explanation: Transit Remand is obtained from a magistrate to legally transport an accused in custody between jurisdictions.

subject: English Language
question: What is the legal process called where witnesses identify an unknown suspect under court/magistrate supervision in jail?
q: What is the legal process called where witnesses identify an unknown suspect under court/magistrate supervision in jail?
explanation: Test Identification Parade (TIP) allows eyewitnesses to formally identify unknown accused individuals.

subject: English Language
question: Which of the following pairings between English election terminology and its technical meaning is INCORRECT?
q: Which of the following pairings between English election terminology and its technical meaning is INCORRECT?

options.text: Polling Booth - Location where voting takes place
Pooling - Process of casting votes in an election

explanation: 'Polling' refers to voting in an election, whereas 'Pooling' means aggregating resources.

subject: English Language
question: What is the meaning of the technical term 'AGROIMPLEMENTS'?
q: What is the meaning of the technical term 'AGROIMPLEMENTS'?
explanation: AGROIMPLEMENTS refers to tools, machinery, and equipment used in farming operations.

subject: English Language
question: What is the correct definition of the term 'Facsimile'?
q: What is the correct definition of the term 'Facsimile'?

options.text: Exact copy or reproduction of a document/image

explanation: A 'Facsimile' is an exact copy or replica of a written or printed document.

subject: English Language
question: Which of the following words is NOT a synonym/equivalent of the word 'General'?
q: Which of the following words is NOT a synonym/equivalent of the word 'General'?
explanation: 'Specific' means particular or unique, which is the antonym of 'General'.

subject: English Language
question: What is the correct financial definition of 'Reimbursement'?
q: What is the correct financial definition of 'Reimbursement'?

options.text: Repayment / refund of expenses incurred earlier
Supply of goods or physical materials
Completion or fulfillment of a task

explanation: 'Reimbursement' refers to paying back money that someone has spent on official expenses.

subject: English Language
question: Which of the following English technical terms is correctly matched with its definition?
q: Which of the following English technical terms is correctly matched with its definition?

options.text: Installation - Setting up or placing equipment/machinery into position

explanation: 'Installation' means setting up an apparatus or system for use.

question: Which of the following English transliterated words is correctly spelt?
q: Which of the following English transliterated words is correctly spelt?

question: Select the correct spelling of the polite request word meaning 'Please' (Kripaya).
q: Select the correct spelling of the polite request word meaning 'Please' (Kripaya).
explanation: 'Kripaya' is the standard spelling for 'Please'.

q: Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error in the sentence: 'How many paise are there in a rupee?'
explanation: 'in one rupee' or 'in a rupee' requires correct articlenoun usage.

question: Which of the following words meaning 'Identity' or 'Harmony' (Tadatmya) is correctly spelt?
q: Which of the following words meaning 'Identity' or 'Harmony' (Tadatmya) is correctly spelt?

subject: English Language
question:
Passage:
The world is a battlefield. In this field, that general achieves victory who recognizes opportunity. He moves forward with as much enthusiasm as opportunity warrants, and falls back with equal alertness during danger. He is the true leader of a nation. But sometimes soldiers enter the field who know how to step forward but do not know how to retreat in crisis. These brave men sacrifice victory for policy. They will erase their army's name, but will not step back. Rarely does such a soldier achieve ultimate victory, but his defeat is more glorious than victory.

Question:
According to the passage, who achieves victory in the world battlefield?
q:
Passage:
The world is a battlefield. In this field, that general achieves victory who recognizes opportunity. He moves forward with as much enthusiasm as opportunity warrants, and falls back with equal alertness during danger. He is the true leader of a nation. But sometimes soldiers enter the field who know how to step forward but do not know how to retreat in crisis. These brave men sacrifice victory for policy. They will erase their army's name, but will not step back. Rarely does such a soldier achieve ultimate victory, but his defeat is more glorious than victory.

Question:
According to the passage, who achieves victory in the world battlefield?

options.text: Those who erase their army's name
Those who sacrifice victory for policy
Those who do not know how to retreat in crisis
Those who recognize opportunity and act at the right time

explanation: The passage states that the general who recognizes opportunity achieves victory.
